title: DRM Catalyst — Descriptor Engineering for Methane Conversion

target.name: CH4_conv_pct
target.description: Observed methane conversion (percent of CH₄ consumed); used only by the scorer.

output.feature_columns: Any number of finite-valued numeric columns; the two files must share the same feature columns in the same order.

feature_descriptions.CH4_conv_pct: Observed methane conversion (percent of CH₄ consumed); present in train rows only.

domain_background: Engineer chemical and materials-informatics descriptors for multi-metal catalysts operating under dry reforming of methane (DRM). The scorer fits a fixed RandomForestRegressor on your descriptors and rewards how much methane-conversion variance your features explain on a held-out set of compositions.
allowed_external_resources: Public materials-informatics, cheminformatics, and machine-learning libraries (scikit-learn, matminer, pymatgen, RDKit, ASE, etc.) and public reference data (atomic-property tables, Materials Project records, OQMD, etc.) are allowed. DFT is permitted in principle but too slow for the hackathon timeline; prefer classical force fields, pre-trained universal ML force fields (MACE-MP-0, CHGNet, M3GNet, ALIGNN-FF), and semi-empirical methods (g-xTB, GFN-xTB) for any first-principles-style descriptors. Do not use hidden reference files or external labels for these specific compositions.
